### Grizz — 2013-09-23T12:49:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-95820>

Caroline,

Sorry, but your Doc will not like what I have to say. IMO, clearly Endos lack common sense, and are blinded by meaningless tests. 1st of all, thyroid measurements are totally distorted by Fluoride, Bromide and goitrogens.

1) TSH measurements are a TOTALLY worthless scam.
[http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/tsh-why-its-useless/](http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/tsh-why-its-useless/)

> Reason #2: The standard thyroid test taught in American medical schools — the TSH test — is among the most inaccurate test in modern medicine, so hypothyroidism is "usually missed," which sets the stage for disease.

[http://w3.newsmax.com/newsletters/brownstein/thyroid\_imbalance.cfm](http://w3.newsmax.com/newsletters/brownstein/thyroid_imbalance.cfm)

> I have seen TSH sometimes go up rather than down while T4 and free T3 did not change or
> may have gone up some. This does not mean that the patient was developing hypothyroidism but
> that the brain was stimulating the body to make more sodium iodide symporters (NIS)…. While
> taking iodide, one may see an elevated TSH but we have to recognize that this is not a bad thing.
> TSH has many actions outside the thyroid that have been discovered…. Often a check of the
> patient’s T4, free T3 and TSH shows the T4 to go down, free T3 going down and TSH going up.
> Iodide is an essential nutrient that is absorbed by all cell lines. Its highest concentration is seen in
> the thyroid. Much more:
> [http://iodineresearch.com/orthoflechas.html](http://iodineresearch.com/orthoflechas.html)

As your body absorbs iodine & gets rid of toxins, your thyroid will become healthier. Contrary to your doctor's BS, how you FEEL is important. Cold hands & cold feet? Endo's usually don't give a hoot about how you feel, and they certainly don't want to discuss the most vital test of all: your body temp graph.
Get into the habit of keeping an accurate temperature graph. This is the measure of your metabolism controlled by your thyroid.
[http://www.drrind.com/therapies/metabolic-temperature-graph](http://www.drrind.com/therapies/metabolic-temperature-graph)

Also, you may need NATURAL thyroid meds if iodine supplements don't make your thyroid recover. Synthetic meds NOT ALLOWED!
[http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/fluoride-and-your-thyroid/](http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/fluoride-and-your-thyroid/)
You can determine this from your body temp chart. The problem being that the thyroid may never recover from Fluoride poisoning. Or may recover very slowly. In either event, you still need iodine for your breasts, ovaries, uterus & brain, and the rest of your body. Never stop taking your iodine.
I find this is the very best reference material for your thyroid:
[http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/site-map/](http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/site-map/)
If your "Doc" does not agree with this document, then get a new doc. There is even a chapter titled how to find a good doctor.

You might like this message I posted last year:
[http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread45205-255.html#post844257](http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread45205-255.html#post844257)
Here is another report you will like:
[http://www.health-science-spirit.com/medicaldisease.html](http://www.health-science-spirit.com/medicaldisease.html)

Bottom line, the 2 most important measurements about your thyroid are:
1) How you feel
2) Body Temp Chart
And other things as defined in Stop Thyroid Madness.

Best to you,

> [endless said:](/goto/post?id=96086)

> I know this has probably been answered a million times in this thread but I just don't have time to go back and read through it all anytime soon....can anyone tell me offhand how many mcg or mg is in a drop of Lugol's 5%? 5% Lugol's solution 1 drop equals 6.25mg in a vertical drop

### Kiera2 — 2013-09-26T00:53:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-96127>

> [Dextery said:](/goto/post?id=96103)
>
> 5% Lugol's solution 1 drop equals 6.25mg in a vertical drop

Yes, that's right Dextery. With a solution of 5% Iodine, 10% Potassium Iodide, one vertical drop = 6.25mg.

In some places this strength solution is called Lugol's 15%, and in others it's called Lugol's 5%.

Please read labels attentively.

### Grizz — 2013-09-28T18:37:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-96400>

**Iodine – The Universal & Holistic Super Mineral by Dr. Cousens**

From a larger physiological perspective, it is important to realize that the thyroid is only one gland of many glands and tissues that needs iodine. Other glands/organs/systems with high Iodine uptake are the breasts, ovaries, cervix, blood, lymph, bones, gastric mucosal, salivary, adrenal, prostate, colon, thymus, lungs, bladder, kidney, and skin. In perspective, the thyroid holds 50 milligrams of iodine, the breasts hold 200 milligrams, the skin holds 400 milligrams of iodine, and the whole body holds 2,000 milligrams, and possibly much more. Iodine is found and used in every hormonal receptor in the body.

There are so many important functions of iodine that it is hard to put them into proper order of importance. The implications of iodine sufficiency go far beyond the levels necessary to avoid cretinism, brain damage, and goiter; and based on the previous cited studies, it is possible that the FDA’s recommended dosage may not even give the minimal protection from these nutritional deficiency diseases.

There are more than twenty-two functions of iodine. These important functions include:

1)Iodine helps synthesize thyroid hormones and prevents both hypo- and hyperthyroidism.

2)Iodine sufficiency reverses hypo- and hyperthyroidism.

3)Iodine supports apoptosis. From a broader perspective, one of iodine’s major functions, in conjunction with oxygen, is to support the process of apoptosis (programmed cell death of unhealthy cells), to protect against cancer and facilitate youthing.

4)Iodine activates hormone receptors and helps prevent certain forms of cancer. The primary cause of cancer, from an energetic perspective, is mitochondrial failure. It is clear that the prevention of cancer, particularly cancer of the breasts, thyroid, ovaries, stomach, and esophagus, is increased with iodine sufficiency. Iodine serves to prevent lipid peroxidation especially in Breast Cancer . Deficiencies of iodine also increase the incidences of breast cancer.

5)Iodine protects ATP function and enhances ATP production.

6)Iodine prevents fibrocystic breast disease.

7)Iodine decreases insulin needs in diabetics.

8)Iodine helps support protein synthesis.

9)Iodine deficiency is a global health threat. Iodine deficiency, in the general population, yields 1-10% cretinism, 5-30% brain damage, and 30-70% loss of energy. In this holistic context, iodine deficiency is a major threat to global health. Iodine deficiency has increased fourfold in the last 40 years and is considered the most common and yet preventable cause of brain damage throughout the world.

10)Iodine destroys pathogens, molds, fungi, parasites, and malaria.

11)Iodine supports immune function.

12)Iodine eliminates toxic halogens from the body (including radioactive I-131). One of the most critical toxic health issues we have today is the exposure of radioactive I-131 from Fukushima. We still have continued and increasing exposure from Fukushima, and unlike Chernobyl, Fukushima has not been sealed off. When the thyroid, as well as the whole body, is filled with healthy iodine (I-127), because of I-127’s similar outer electron shell, in higher saturation levels, it can provide significant protection of vulnerable receptor sites from uptake of radioactive I-131 and also push out radioactive I-131 already present. This is known as competitive inhibition, and explains why Chernobyl survivors who took iodine, even after the nuclear fallout, escaped thyroid cancer.

13)Iodine regulates estrogen production in the ovaries.

14)Iodine is anti-mucolytic (meaning it reduces mucus catarrh).

15)Iodine neutralizes hydroxyl ions and hydrates the cells.

16)Iodine makes us smarter. As previously mentioned iodine helps with mental functioning. Low iodine is associated with low IQ’s with a difference of up to 13.5 points as cited in the Bleichrodt study of 1994 in children; but iodine deficiency is also associated with mental functioning in adults, because iodine not only chelates lead, but, according to Dr. Jorge Flechas, iodine prevents lead from lodging in the body in the first place. This occurs if enough iodine is supplemented, as discussed above, in order to push out fluoride, a culprit responsible for lead accumulation in the body. Low thyroid function decreases brain circulation, which slows intellectual function. Dr. Steven Langer in the book Solved: The Riddle of Illness, points out that low thyroid function is associated with cognitive impairment, memory loss, depression, slowness of mind, anxiety, suicidal tendencies, and a variety of psychiatric disorders. Bleichrodt, in 1994, did a meta-analysis looking at 17 studies showing iodine sufficiency increases IQ by 13.5 points in children.

17)Iodine prevents heart disease

18)Iodine is needed with the use of cordless phones, Cell Phone s and now smart meters to prevent hypothyroidism.

19)Iodine supports pregnancy (as the fetus undergoes more apoptosis than any other developmental stage).

20)High doses of iodine may be used to reverse certain diseases. At 6 grams daily (which is 6 million micrograms/day or 6,000 milligrams/day!), a much higher dose, iodine has been used to cure syphilis, skin lesions, and chronic lung disease.

21)High doses of iodine may be used for wounds, bedsores, inflammatory and traumatic pain,
**_and restoration of hair growth when applied topically_**. ???
Iodine has many other uses, including relieving headaches and eliminating toenail fungus.

Is this for real??? Grizz

22)Iodine helps in the diminishing of tissue scarring, cheloid formations, and Dupuytren’s and Peyronie’s contractures, which are hyper-scarring conditions.
**DR. COUSENS' BLOG - Iodine**
[http://www.gabrielcousens.com/DRCOU...–-The-Universal--Holistic-Super-Mineral-.aspx](http://www.gabrielcousens.com/DRCOUSENS/DRCOUSENSBLOG/tabid/364/PostID/169/language/en-US/Iodine-%E2%80%93-The-Universal--Holistic-Super-Mineral-.aspx)

### Grizz — 2013-09-28T19:15:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-96404>

> [healthman said:](/goto/post?id=96394)
>
> Hi
> I am new to this forum and have a question regarding Iodine and home testing, I am based in the UK and have found this test online, can anyone tell me what the best test is to get and where to purchase it in the UK.
>
> I am 35, male and also have low testosterone in which I am trying to correct. I am currently on 9 drops of nascent iodine per day.
> Thanks

Healthman,

**Nascent is not Recommended**
i. Nascent (aka Magnascent, Detoxified, or Atomidine) – Based on
Edgar Cayce’s “energized” iodine. The iodine is reduced to a 1% concentration in 100% ethyl alcohol and electro-magnetically transmuted (while being suspended in a wet bath containing a mild acid solution) into the Atomic state. 1 drop = 150 microgram (mcg) or .150 mg of Iodine. This form will not saturate body tissues and has not been proven to detox halides such as bromide, fluoride, chloride, and mercury, as other forms have. This form of iodine is not recommended on this protocol as the goal is tissue saturation and halide detoxification / protection.

Your 9 drops a day ( less then 1.5mg) are not even close to supplying your body with with needed iodine.

**Recommended Forms**
i. Lugol’s liquid – Iodine / Potassium Iodide – 2% and 5% solutions. 2%
solution is 2.5 mgs / drop. 5% solution is 6.25 mgs / drop.
ii. Iodoral – Lugol’s formula in pill form – Iodine / Potassium Iodide - 12.5
mgs & 50 mgs
iii. Biotics Research Iodizyme – 12.5 mgs per tablet of Iodine / Iodide
iv. Tri-Iodine by Vitaminlife – 12.5 mgs per tablet of Iodine / Iodide.
[http://home.comcast.net/~jocy1/junk/The Guide to Supplementing with Iodine.pdf](http://home.comcast.net/~jocy1/junk/The%20Guide%20to%20Supplementing%20with%20Iodine.pdf)

We need both iodine AND Potassium Iodide for our body, and the best choices are Lugols liquid & iodoral capsules. Iodine will indeed increase your testosterone as the testes & prostate both need iodine.

See this report for men:
[http://curezone.com/forums/fm.asp?i=1955837#i](http://curezone.com/forums/fm.asp?i=1955837#i)

The best iodine urine testing is here. Also do the bromide & fluoride test: I think they ship to Europe.
[http://www.hakalalabs.com/testing.html](http://www.hakalalabs.com/testing.html)
UK Testing
[http://purehealthshop.co.uk/shop/article\_117/Iodine-Loading-Test.html?pse=apq](http://purehealthshop.co.uk/shop/article_117/Iodine-Loading-Test.html?pse=apq).
[http://www.biolab.co.uk/index.php/cmsid\_\_biolab\_test/Iodine\_-\_urine](http://www.biolab.co.uk/index.php/cmsid__biolab_test/Iodine_-_urine)

IMO, if you have not been eating a lot seafoods & sea Veggies every day then you are deficient in iodine, and it is a waste of money to do the urine test to tell you that you are deficient. Titrate up to 50mg a day of lugols or iodoral with required co-supplements for a year, then do your urine loading test. Follow the Dr. Browstein Iodine Protocol here:
[http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout)

PS) Stop taking iodine supplements 1 week before doing the Urine Loading test.

See this link for buying Lugols in Europe or making your own for pennies on the dollar. You can also make a quart of 5% Lugols ($800 worth) for only $60:
[https://docs.google.com/document/d/...vS0jH9bkeQK\_KzvG4/edit#heading=h.emm10vkwenqy](https://docs.google.com/document/d/16Dpy1_mGUy3dOxSpr7QY4oeiDVvS0jH9bkeQK_KzvG4/edit#heading=h.emm10vkwenqy)

Grizz

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-96443>

> [healthman said:](/goto/post?id=96439)

> Also Lugols Iodine - from healthleadsuk

Healthman,
Since you have been taking Nascent for a while, I suggest you start with 1 drop of lugols a day in a glass of water and titrate up to 4 drops over 4 weeks, and then switch to iodoral and titrate up to 50mg over the next 4 weeks. If you get detox symptoms,
[http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout)
do the salt flush & pulse dosing.
[http://tinyurl.com/Detox-Help](http://tinyurl.com/Detox-Help)

If it feels right for you, you might titrate up faster. Everyone is different, and some have even reported minimal detox starting with 50mg. Some had had extreme difficulty getting past 3mg and have been so toxic that they had to cut their dosage to to 1/10th of a drop to begin titrating. There is no perfect answer that fits everyone.

Let us know which approach works best for you,
Grizz

### Kiera2 — 2013-10-10T06:29:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-97410>

> [endless said:](/goto/post?id=97376)
>
> I just bought a new bottle of Lugol's from a different source. It doesn't tell the strength anywhere on the bottle, but I called the company and was told it is 5% iodine and 10% potassium iodide. Does that mean it is 15%? If so I just gave myself a whopping dose  Anyone know?

That is the standard/original strength.

With a solution of 5% Iodine, 10% Potassium Iodide, one vertical drop = 6.25mg.

In some places this strength solution is called Lugol's 15%, and in others it's called Lugol's 5%.

### Sara1 — 2013-10-10T07:01:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-97411>

.
Quick Question

Iodine you're taking must be bound to a salt - such as Potassium. That double bond is nearly impossible for the body to break apart. Which leaves the iodine in a toxic form. Nascent Iodine is in the atomic form & is easy for the body to use.

I don't like the work of Dr Brownstein - he claims more than RDA of iodine is dangerous. Which isn't true. I prefer the work of Dr Mark Sircus who uses high doses of Nascent Iodine in his latest book. Dr Sircus also recommends Mg-BiCarbonate - I've found to be a huge help.

However, Nascent Iodine has it's draw backs. Dr Edward Group III ND DC - developed Nascent Iodine bound to Glycerin not ETOH. His product is developed in a method that creates a magnetic charge. His website can explain it better than I can. [http://www.globalhealingcenter.com/nascent-iodine-detoxadine.html](http://www.globalhealingcenter.com/nascent-iodine-detoxadine.html)
Dr Group's Iodine is the only one proven to show Tissue Saturation. His product is plant based (kelp) not chemical as found in all other Iodine products.

Lugols and Triquench Iodine are toxic & not safe to take from what I understand. I'm using Dr Group's Iodine and have found great success in a short period of time. Amazing difference.

\[video=youtube;729oMkA8LGE\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=729oMkA8LGE\[/video\]

Iodine you're taking must be bound to a salt - such as Potassium. That double bond is nearly impossible for the body to break apart. Which leaves the iodine in a toxic form. Nascent Iodine is in the atomic form & is easy for the body to use.

I don't like the work of Dr Brownstein - he claims more than RDA of iodine is dangerous. Which isn't true. I prefer the work of Dr Mark Sircus who uses high doses of Nascent Iodine in his latest book. Dr Sircus also recommends Mg-BiCarbonate - I've found to be a huge help.

However, Nascent Iodine has it's draw backs. Dr Edward Group III ND DC - developed Nascent Iodine bound to Glycerin not ETOH. His product is developed in a method that creates a magnetic charge. His website can explain it better than I can. [http://www.globalhealingcenter.com/nascent-iodine-detoxadine.html](http://www.globalhealingcenter.com/nascent-iodine-detoxadine.html)
Dr Group's Iodine is the only one proven to show Tissue Saturation. His product is plant based (kelp) not chemical as found in all other Iodine products.

### Grizz — 2013-10-11T22:30:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-97551>

I've just stumbled into another amazing testimonial:

> "I am now a true Iodine believer. I suffered for over 30 years with PCOS, debilitating IBS, chronic allergies and upper respiratory infections,(including six bouts of pneumonia and many more of bronchitis and sinusitis), widespread eczema, fibrocystic breast disease, chronic fatigue and other hypothyroid-like symptoms such as weight gain and depression, asthma, heart arrythmias, and many other minor and chronic issues, finally culminating in Breast Cancer five years ago, and the removal of one ovary due to severe cysts. At one point, a doctor explained to me that we would be looking for one cause for all the problems, rather than many problems in the same body, which made sense. So needless to say, I went from doctor-to-doctor for years, searching for the underlying cause of all these many problems, with not much more than a pat on the hand and a lecture to watch my diet and get more exercise. In the meantime, my marriage, family and entire life were seriously impacted. I finally stumbled across the concept of iodine supplementation on the web — a simple pop-up ad did for me what all those doctors and their fancy degrees could not. Looking at the list of possible symptoms of iodine deficiency was like looking at my own medical history, and I couldn’t start taking it fast enough. After one month on 50mg of iodine, my IBS disappeared, after over 30 years of daily misery. And I mean DISAPPEARED. It was like a miracle for me. My energy picked up significantly. My skin stopped flaking, my hair became shiny again, and I haven’t broken a nail in three months — I’ve never experienced that in my whole life. I have to file them down weekly! My eczema is greatly improved. My one remaining breast is smoother, and less painful around my period. My allergies are also significantly improved, and getting better. My heartbeat is much more regular now. In short, I have seen improvement or complete resolution of nearly ALL of my symptoms, and the improvements continue, after four months now. It truly is amazing. The impact that this small amount of iodine has had on my health cannot be overlooked or overstated. My one continuing hope is that I’ll lose some weight, and I believe that it’s only a matter of time.
>
> It’s really a tragedy that most doctors still refuse to believe that iodine can help with these kinds of problems, and, indeed, are afraid to supplement for fear of poisoning their patients. I’ve never felt better in my whole life, taking the kind of dose that many warn will cause a toxic overdose! Ignorance abounds, and I see so many women who clearly suffer from iodine deficiency, and will likely suffer for the rest of their lives, because of this ignorance. I’m so glad I found my own answers, and I hope this will help others to do the same!
> [http://www.paleoplan.com/2012/09-10/do-you-need-to-supplement-iodine/#comment-186705](http://www.paleoplan.com/2012/09-10/do-you-need-to-supplement-iodine/#comment-186705)

Does anyone else have an iodine testimonial to contribute?
Grizz

### agatha — 2013-10-21T17:51:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-98507>

Hello all,

I haven't posted in a while but wanted to feed back about great results with iodine. I have been doing one drop of Lugol's in a glass of water every fortnight together with some seaweed in diet. My goal was to eliminate severe premenstrual breast pain. I have kept the dosage on the lower side because of worries about triggering Hashimoto's

It worked!!! The pain is gone together with other symptoms of PMS (spots, grumpiness) and my fingernails are stronger too. I have also been able to reduce my thyroid meds a notch.
Thanks everyone here for flagging this up.
Agatha

1) Did he take the exact Dr. Brownstein Protocol?
\* Â½ tsp Natural Celtic Salt, Himalayan Pink Salt, Redmonds Salt or Hawaiian black salt. If the sea salt is pure white, it is unacceptable (minerals have been stripped out )
\* 200+ mcg selenium (L-selenomethionine preferred)
\* 400 mg Magnesium - best=glycinate, citrate or malate others are poorly absorbed -calcium supplements if used, > 2,000mg daily will purge magnesium
\* 2,000+ mg Vitamin C - Ester-C preferred (ascorbic acid may upset stomach)
\* ATP Cofactors caplet contains both 100mg B2 & 500mg non-flushing B3 in the correct ratio. Take 1/2 caplet w/iodine up to 25mg, & Â½ caplet for each 25mg of iodine. Can be discontinued after 1 yr, if iodine sufficient & no deficiency or detox symptoms
[http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout)

If he took iodine without the specified selenium, L-selenomethionine, then the answer is YES. Iodine without selenium can indeed damage the thyroid. Iodine & selenium must always be taken together. Each of the specified co-supplements must be taken to engage the iodide symporters. Did he take the required Lugols or Iodoral? Not all forms of iodine are acceptable to Dr. Brownstein. Did he start with a LOW dose of iodine and titrate slowly to avoid too much detox?

**an over active nodule by Dr. Dach:**
(Beware, the knife happy doctors may suggest cancer, so read thes carefully)
You might ask the obvious question, "Thyroid nodules are found in 67% of the population. What is causing this?" I would suggest that the most likely explanation is subclinical iodine deficiency in the population. Iodine deficiency causes thyroid enlargement (goiter), thyroid nodules, and thyroid cancer.

Thyroid cancer appears linked to Iodine deficiency in both animal models and humans.
Studies have shown that iodine deficiency is associated with increased anaplastic thyroid cancer, the aggressive type unresposive to treatment and associated with high mortality rate. Population studies in which iodine supplementation was given showed reduced mortality from thyroid cancer. Incidentally, this reduced mortality was also associated with an increase in well differentiated papillary cancers, again suggesting the papillary type to be associated with a better outcome.
Make sure to take Iodine suppplements to reduce the risk of dangerous types of thyroid cancer. For more on Iodine and cancer prevention
[http://jeffreydach.com/2009/08/16/the-thyroid-nodule-epidemic-by-jeffrey-dach-md.aspx](http://jeffreydach.com/2009/08/16/the-thyroid-nodule-epidemic-by-jeffrey-dach-md.aspx)

**iodine isn't stored in the body?**
Iodine is required by every cell in the body, and is stored in every cell. Especially the thyroid, female organs & the prostate+testes. If the body doesn't get enough iodine, then deficiency disease occurs, such as swollen thyroid (goiter), Hypothyroid, Fibrocystic Breast Disease, Ovary Cysts, & uterus problems. [http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Deficiency](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Deficiency)

All deficiency disease are also detailed in "Recommended Reading" by subject:
[http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Reading](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Reading)

**isn't toxic?**
Iodine is proven safe & effective:

> I eat 3 Brazil nuts a day for selenium. Is this sufficient or would I have to take selenium in pill form?

@@ Veelansu,
I also eat 3 Brazil Nuts a day for my selenium. I "assume" it is OK, and I have not had any problems. We could also eat a can of sardines for selenium. However I am not the doctor, and I can't give advice. Dr. Brownstein is very clear about the dose of selenium we should take. The problem with substituting foods is that we just don't know how much selenium is in those foods. There could be insufficient amounts depending on the soil conditions where the nuts are grown. To be on the safe side, I have to officially recommend what the doctor says.

### Grizz — 2013-11-03T09:34:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-99710>

> [Rylee said:](/goto/post?id=99691)
>
> Grizz - I've read the following link provided by Lynne Farrow on Facebook:
>
> [http://jeffreydachmd.com/hashimotos-selenium-and-iodine-part-two/](http://jeffreydachmd.com/hashimotos-selenium-and-iodine-part-two/)
>
> Under the heading "Renato Iwakura Reviews the Literature", it states that this iodine expert believes potassium iodide is safer than iodine found in seaweed because seaweed contains heavy metals and halides. I know Iodorol and Lugol's contain a combination of potassium iodide and iodine. Is there any danger in consuming the "iodine"? Would it be safer just to take potassium iodide?
> I wanted to ask you because you've done so much research. Thanks for all your help on this thread!

@@ Rylee,
The human body requires both iodine and Potassium iodide. That is why Lugols & Iodoral were invented. They contain both in the correct ratio:

> \* Our body requires both iodine AND iodide (lugols iodine & iodoral capsules recommended)
> \- - - Iodine is used by the prostate + Stomach
> \- - - Iodide (SSKI) is used by the thyroid + skin + Salivary
> \- - - kidneys, spleen, liver, blood, breasts & intestines can use either form
> Thyroid needs 3% prefers iodide
> Skin needs 20% and prefers iodide
> Salivary Glands prefer iodide
> Muscles (650mg) and fat needs(700mg iodine) 70%
> Breast tissue needs 3-5 mg / day minimum and prefers iodine
> Prostate prefers iodine
> Stomach prefers iodine
> Lungs prefer iodide
> Entire body stores 1500 mg 1.5 gm total
> [http://tinyurl.com/iodine-instructions](http://tinyurl.com/iodine-instructions)
> Also see my research on seaweed in this link.

@@ Caroline,
Sorry, but I would not want to speculate on the accuracy of one test vs any other test. The iodine doctors only recommend the Urine Loading Test:
[http://www.hakalalabs.com/testing.html](http://www.hakalalabs.com/testing.html)

I am not a doctor, so I can't offer a worthwhile opinion on this. To remain credible, I must only forward the Iodine Doctor's advice as best I can. I do think any iodine test is better than no testing. Even the lowly skin patch test has some value. When I first started iodine, my skin patch test disappeared in less than 3 minutes ! Now the patch lasts about 6 hours, so that must indicate I was grossly deficient. The gold standard in iodine testing remains the Urine Loading Test, which is even more valuable now that it includes Fluoride & Bromide testing as an option.

**Urine Spot test explained, in comparison to other tests:**
[http://www.celluliteinvestigation.com/2010/10/3-tests-for-iodine-deficiency.html](http://www.celluliteinvestigation.com/2010/10/3-tests-for-iodine-deficiency.html)

[More on iodine testing](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1_q9ixGwZFon9yidvmZ0IFbp3Vj5jXDmOp0HwpdBS6VY/edit#heading=h.qt5k4vox5clg)

Hope this helps,
Grizz

### Grizz — 2013-11-09T18:15:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-100272>

> [Jasmin said:](/goto/post?id=100244)

> My problem is that my metabolism has fallen.... before my metabolism breaks down

Jasmin,

I know I will get some flack for this, but here it is anyway:
1) TSH is a totally worthless measurement
[http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/tsh-why-its-useless/](http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/tsh-why-its-useless/)

2) Metabolism should be measured with a chart & thermometer. Chart your body temp. If your body temp is falling, you need more Natural Thyroid Hormone ( NOT synthroid). If your body temp gets above normal, then reduce your thyroid meds. ( some special rules apply to women's cycle for body temp )
[http://www.drrind.com/therapies/metabolic-temperature-graph](http://www.drrind.com/therapies/metabolic-temperature-graph)

3) Your body absolutely requires iodine for your breasts, ovaries, uterus, brain & thyroid and for a healthy smart baby if you get pregnant. So it is a mistake to quit iodine. Just try reducing your iodine dosage down to 6mg a day until you get control of your metabolism using natural thyroid meds.

The problem is that damage to your thyroid from Bromide, Fluoride & Chlorine can take YEARS to recover.
[http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/fluoride-and-your-thyroid/](http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/fluoride-and-your-thyroid/)
In some severe cases, it may never recover & you will need natural thyroid meds for life.

But you still need your iodine for the rest of your body. Iodine prevents fibrocystic breasts, ovary cysts, uterus problems and related cancers, & is required for hormonal balance. Read the book, The Iodine Crises to see how the author cured her own breast cancer with iodine:
[http://www.amazon.com/The-Iodine-Cr...=1363367455&sr=8-1&keywords=the+iodine+crises](http://www.amazon.com/The-Iodine-Crisis-About-Wreck/dp/098603200X/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1363367455&sr=8-1&keywords=the+iodine+crises)
I bought this book for everyone in my church and others in my neighborhood.

Never use Synthroid, T4 Meds don't work:
[http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/t4-only-meds-dont-work/](http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/t4-only-meds-dont-work/)

A woman taking iodine can have BRILLIANT babies without ADD, iodine is required for healthy fetus growth, especially for brain development:
[http://www.youtube.com/watch?feature=player\_embedded&v=uc4Q3kzBSc4](http://www.youtube.com/watch?feature=player_embedded&v=uc4Q3kzBSc4)

Hope this helps,
Grizz

### Grizz — 2013-11-11T11:26:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-100410>

> [Jasmin said:](/goto/post?id=100403)
>
> 1\. Grizz I agree that TSH is a totally worthless measurement - my concerne is that my T4 has fallen after I started Lugol's.
>
> 2\. I will start beasure my body temp today - thank you for reminding me.
>
> 3\. I have reduced my dosage down to 2 drops of Lugol's a day. I will have my T3 and T4 measured in about 3 weeks and keep my fingers crossed
>
> I will never use Synthroid - hope that I can keep my Erfa Thyroid but I am at a pretty high dose now - 4 grains - and perhaps I need to upp the dose...
>
> Just got one more question. The first point at link you gave me "Top 12 Ways to Make your Body More Alkaline" said that "Start your day with a large glass of water with the juice of a whole, freshly-squeezed lemon." Can I do that at the same time as I take Lugol's....I think that I have read somewhere that Iodine is not to be taken with C-vitamin. Lemons have a lot of c-vitamin but is it something that reduce the absorption of Lugol's? Or can I drink the freshly-squeezed lemon at an other time of the day? (hope it is not a stupid question).
>
> Thank you so much for your time and your hard work

Jasmin,

No such thing as stupid questions. You are right. Vitamin C should not be mixed with Lugols. However it can be mixed with iodoral tablets. The vitamin C converts the iodide into iodine.

The reason for adding the lemon to water is it raises the body's pH. The lemon can be added to water at any time of the day.

T4 falling after starting iodine . . . . We have an identical question at the Curezone. Here is their expert answer:
[http://curezone.com/forums/fm.asp?i=2122623#i](http://curezone.com/forums/fm.asp?i=2122623#i)
[http://curezone.com/forums/fm.asp?i=2122730#i](http://curezone.com/forums/fm.asp?i=2122730#i)

As we can see, the answer is complex, involving the ratio of iodine & selenium, and general body nutrition.
Such a tiny little organ can be soooo fussy and cause such problems, it is amazing. Also agonizingly complex, like a Rubic Cube !

**T4 - Why it may be low**:
Medications that can affect your T4 levels include:
drugs including hormones, such as androgen, estrogen, birth control pills
drugs designed to affect your thyroid or treat thyroid conditions <===== !
some drugs designed to treat cancer
steroids
[http://www.healthline.com/health/t4-test](http://www.healthline.com/health/t4-test)

Could iodine could influence T4 test results just because Iodine helps the thyroid? In other words do we expect to see T4 low as iodine is doing its job?

> Yes taking iodine affects the test results:
> ...It is also important to mention if you have had any X-ray tests that have used a special contrast dye as this may contain iodine which can affect the test results....
> [http://www.patient.co.uk/health/thyroid-function-tests#](http://www.patient.co.uk/health/thyroid-function-tests#)

> The thyroid gland needs iodine to synthesize T4 and T3. Whole body iodine sufficiency is a critical means to counter the side effects of thyroid hormone medications. Long-term use of these drugs is associated with depletion of thyroid and tissue iodine levels, as well as increased rates of cancer. All thyroid patients should be on Iodine Therapy. Selenium along with iodine is essentially important for T4 to T3 conversion in the liver. It is important to supplement with Selenium for any kind of thyroid problem.
> [http://joannebrophy.com/programs/iodinetherapy.htm](http://joannebrophy.com/programs/iodinetherapy.htm)

So clearly taking iodine at the same time as testing your thyroid will give inaccurate T4 results. I would try stopping both selenium AND iodine and any other items in the list above 2 weeks or more prior to testing to get a more accurate test result of your normal thyroid function. What about taking your thyroid meds? Ask your doctor if you should stop taking your thyroid meds prior to testing. Let me know what he says.

This begs a far more important question. Does your doctor even recommend that you take iodine supplements? If not, maybe you need to find a new Doc. Everyone knows that iodine is required for the thyroid and female organ health. Then why do some doctors discourage taking iodine that the body requires? WHAT IS WRONG WITH THEM ? ? ? ? I think there is something grossly wrong with any doctor that is against iodine supplementation. Why isn't your Doc answering your question?

When in doubt always consult with STTM: Here is what they say about iodine:
[http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/search/iodine](http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/search/iodine)
Their index:
[http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/site-map/](http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/site-map/)

STTM is the undisputed expert source for thyroid questions.

I'm afraid I wasn't much help, if I discover something brilliant, I'll post it here,
Grizz

Most important get him off of ALL wheat products and all packaged foods. Problems with high blood pressure & statins are directly related to wheat & packaged foods. Nothing with high fructose corn syrup, no artificial oils, no foods with hydrogenated oil. He must exercise, I recommend walking 2 miles a day or better. Get him the book, **[Wheat Belly](http://www.wheatbellyblog.com/)** so he can see why wheat causes so many health problems. Also buy him the book, **[Wheat Brain.](http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread60029-8.html#post1365144)**

### Grizz — 2013-11-22T08:37:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-101319>

Dr. Buist message at her Yahoo Iodine Group:

> Iodine and human nutrition. How much iodine do we need? What are the best sources? What happens if we don't get enough iodine?
>
> Iodine has been effective with hypothyroid and hyperthyroid conditions, goiter, fibrocystic breasts, uterine fibroids, ovarian cysts, brain fog, constipation, obesity, diabetes, cancer, high blood pressure, arrhythmias like atrial fibrillation, and detoxification issues (mercury, perchlorate, fluoride, bromide).
>
> Iodine is in every cell in the body and is essential in pregnancy. It is found in high levels in the thyroid gland, breast, ovaries, prostate, liver, lung, heart, stomach, and adrenal glands.
>
> There is currently a great deal of research on iodine and cancer, including cancer of the breast, colon, lungs, ovaries, pancreas, prostate, stomach, and thyroid.
>
> Some doctors are now using iodine in the treatment of children with down syndrome as well as autism with positive results.
>
> If you an an Iodine Researcher, you may be interested in our subgroup, IodineResearch: [Yahoo Groups](http://tech.groups.yahoo.com/group/IodineResearch/)
>
> About the owner: I am a 13 yr thyroid cancer survivor saved by iodine. Treated with 3 rounds of RAI and still had registering Tg markers. In 2006 when my markers rose my endo recommended external beam radition. I sought the help of Dr. David Brownstein - one of the pioneers in iodine. He placed me on 50 mgs of iodine, detoxed, balanced my hormones, switched me from Synthroid to Armour and started a nutritional balancing program. I removed 11 amalgams and a root canal tooth. ### Grizz — 2013-12-09T12:15:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-102954>

Radio Interview with Lynne Farrow, author of [The Iodine Crises.](http://www.amazon.com/Iodine-Crisis-What-About-Wreck/dp/098603200X/ref=sr_1_1?s=books&ie=UTF8&qid=1386583815&sr=1-1&keywords=the+iodine+crises)

[Radio interview: The Iodine Crisis by Lynne Farrow](http://lynnefarrow.net/radioshow.html)

**Also FASCINATING Book Reviews for her book:**
[http://lynnefarrow.net/reviews.html](http://lynnefarrow.net/reviews.html)

**Testimonial for Lynn Farrow's Book:**
Severe Brain Fog cured by iodine:

> As a Clinical Social Worker (2 grad degrees, one in psychotherapy and medical social work) I really did try everything. I have lived in sunny California most of my life and when I moved to Fairbanks, Alaska I was NOT prepared for the
> intense darkness of Winter. We are the "Land of the Midnight Sun (almost 24 hours in the Summer months, but the opposite part is "Land of the Noon-Time Moon" for at least 3 or 4 months in the Winter time. I tried sun-light boxes for 22 years; I actually fell asleep against one for over an hour and a half!
>
> I have had a private practice since 1990 and fell asleep on at least 2 patients during their counseling sessions!! I tried to count my yawns in one day and it was over 50. I was constantly apologizing to my patients for yawning and explaining that I wasn't that I was bored with their story! I felt like the proverbial joke about someone desperately calling a suicide hotline and being told they have to be put on hold!
>
> I would drive home in the Arctic Winter dark and cry much of the way because I was convinced I would eventually hit a car and kill a family. I sang loudly; I'd scream repeatedly in an effort to up my adrenalin. I often turned the radio way up, opened the window even at 40 below zero trying to shock myself awake. I dug my fingernails into my palms trying to cause pain that would startle me, bit my lips... nothing happened. I begged my doctor for answers. I even asked for Electric Shock Treatment. (I had referred a patient for ECT years before. I knew the research.) She was sympathetic but no go.
>
> She decided to give me a high dose of Ritalin. I took 2 - 20mg sustained release Ritalin a day with NO impact. I ATE COFFEE BEANS with the 40 mg of Ritalin; I thought if I chewed the whole bean and swallowed it I'd get the most caffeine out of them, but no change.) I was sleepwalking through the whole winter and constantly afraid of killing people in my car.
>
> Then a neuropsych friend in Canada mentioned the work of Dr. David Brownstein with Iodine and told me to try Iodoral as a little pick-me-up for the tough Arctic Winters. He didn't know about my struggles. I RAN to the local health food place, bought the book and the Iodoral. About 3 weeks in to taking increasing amounts I literally got up on a Saturday and was stunned to feel my brain come out of the Fog.It was rather dramatic.
>
> Turns out that if you have low thyroid Iodine can make a huge difference. Well, I have been on synthetic thyroid and natural, also, but no change. Iodine has changed my life!! I told my doctors and got no response. I have told anyone who will listen and many of my patients are now taking it with amazing results. Of all people, my dentist listened to me and his whole extended family are taking it now.
>
> A wonderful extra surprise from the iodine is that throughout my whole life I have had a struggle recalling names. Once the light-bulb went off 3 weeks into the Iodoral titration I remembered almost everyone, even their dog's name or things about their boyfriends just from our conversation!
>
> I am very grateful to have a life. It has been 3 winters without ANY struggle. And, I don't even YAWN! Who knew iodine would do that? I realized I had stopped yawning when in April after the first winter on iodine I suddenly yawned and realized it was my first yawn since taking iodine. Oh my! How can my doctor hear my story and not care? I have given her the book by Brownstein and she is not interested. I truly believe it saved my life, either from a terrible car accident or suicide.
>
> Hope this info helps you get the word out. Feel free to use my name and credentials. Thank you so much!
>
> K. R., MSW, ACSW, DCSW, LCSW
> [http://lynnefarrow.net/mentalhealth.html](http://lynnefarrow.net/mentalhealth.html)

Grizz

### nicld — 2013-12-09T20:53:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-102992>

Quick question, will taking say 12.5 to 25 mg of iodine cause one's TSH to go through the roof?

DD's TSH is over 11 (still waiting for more test results) and one of her docs said that iodine could cause it.

### Grizz — 2013-12-09T22:08:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-103003>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=102992)
>
> Quick question, will taking say 12.5 to 25 mg of iodine cause one's TSH to go through the roof?
>
> DD's TSH is over 11 (still waiting for more test results) and one of her docs said that iodine could cause it.

nicld,
There is no such thing as a quick question where the thyroid is concerned. It should be quick & easy but everyone makes it insanely overly complicated. I will cut through all the BS fir you.
**TSH is a worthless, meaningless test, Don't waste your time & money:**
[http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/tsh-why-its-useless/](http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/tsh-why-its-useless/)
[http://iodineresearch.com/orthoflechas.html](http://iodineresearch.com/orthoflechas.html)
[http://w3.newsmax.com/newsletters/brownstein/thyroid\_imbalance.cfm](http://w3.newsmax.com/newsletters/brownstein/thyroid_imbalance.cfm)

Regardless of the lies your Doctor (Drug Pusher) may tell you, Iodine is absolutely REQUIRED for your Thyroid health. Our thyroid was never intended to receive thyroid drugs. It was intended to run on its own power with iodine rich seafoods. Without iodine, your thyroid can swell with goiter, or thyroid nodules. The **REAL problems** we have with our thyroid **are what your doctor won't talk about.** The toxins of bromide, fluoride & chlorine will displace the required iodine in our thyroid, and can actually kill it off, thereby forcing you to take thyroid drugs. See this description for why:
[http://www.freegrab.net/Iodine update.htm#section18](http://www.freegrab.net/Iodine%20update.htm#section18)

We are required to take high dosages of iodine to displace the toxins bromide, fluoride & chlorine in our organs. This is why we get "Detox Symptoms" when supplementing with iodine. Also why the co-supplements are required to open the detox pathways. If you are a woman, your breasts, ovaries & uterus also REQUIRE a lot of iodine, or else you get fibrocystic breast disease, ovary cysts, & severe uterus problems & unbalanced hormones. This is why women develop hypothyroid 90% more than men. All of the female organs are competing for the little iodine that we get in our "Standard American Diet." This is also why the female organs are the first to get cancers, caused by iodine deficiency. Yes, Iodine is used by our immune system to kill cancer cells.

But the answer is yes, iodine does make TSH go up, but that is a good thing as described in the above links. You must buy bromide free bread, stop drinking fluoridated water, and try your best to eliminate the toxins in your food to help protect your thyroid & female organs. Men also suffer from iodine deficiency with theor prostate & falling testosterone as they get older.

I suggest that the only thyroid testing that you need to do is to measure your body temp with a chart.
[http://www.drrind.com/therapies/metabolic-temperature-graph](http://www.drrind.com/therapies/metabolic-temperature-graph)
If your body temp is below normal, then you are hypothyroid, regardless of what your doctor thyroid testing says. Doctors don't recognize hypothyroid unless it bites them in the nose, they are so confused over their blood testing numbers. Thyroid testing is a bunch of BS just to make $$$ for your doctor & his drug salesman. The entire purpose of the thyroid is to regulate your metabolism (body temp). If your body temp is still lower than normal after taking iodine for several months, then you may need to take some natural thyroid hormone temporarily until your thyroid is able to recover. When your thyroid starts recovering, you will need to lower your dosage of natural thyroid hormone as your body temp gets higher than normal.

References:
[http://tinyurl.com/iodine-instructions](http://tinyurl.com/iodine-instructions)
[http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references](http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references)
See hypothyroid, hyperthyroid, thyroid, TSH, cancer
[http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Reading](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Reading)

Also read the Thyroid Bible:
[http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/site-map/](http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/site-map/)
Don't let your doctor take advantage of you. Always consult with our Bible to keep him honest.

PS) There are some special rules to observe for women to measure body temp depending on menstrual cycle times. Women are not always 98.6 degrees.
Hope this helps on the overly complicated subject of the thyroid,

Grizz

### Grizz — 2013-12-09T23:33:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-103006>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=103005)

Jansz,
I don't have any information for low Temp in men when progesterone is low. You or Dr. Kruse are probably the experts on this subject.

Antibodies present in the thyroid tests require a doctor's attention, and indicate the immune system is attacking the thyroid, resuting in Hashimotos disease. In our Bible, how to determine if you have it:
[http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/hashimotos/](http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/hashimotos/)

What causes the thyroid inflammation? One reason might be low iodine, as well as poor levels of certain minerals such as selenium, zinc, and iron. Iodine cannot be taken without selenium, as specified by Dr. Brownstein. (IMO, most iodine doctors agree that iodine deficiency is the major reason for Hashimotos )

Additional Complete references on Hashi;
[https://docs.google.com/document/d/...5jXDmOp0HwpdBS6VY/edit#heading=h.8eltjvzb6ets](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1_q9ixGwZFon9yidvmZ0IFbp3Vj5jXDmOp0HwpdBS6VY/edit#heading=h.8eltjvzb6ets)

My own opinion, hashimotos can also be caused by wheat causing the immune system to go berserk. Wheat can cause a number of autoimmune diseases. See complete details in the book, Wheat Belly by Dr. Davis.
**Dr. Davis on Hashi:**
How to Give Yourself Hashimoto’s Thyroiditis: 101
[http://blog.trackyourplaque.com/2009/01/how-to-give-yourself-hashimotos-thyroiditis-101.html](http://blog.trackyourplaque.com/2009/01/how-to-give-yourself-hashimotos-thyroiditis-101.html)

**Dr. Dach Selenium on Hashi**
[http://jeffreydach.com/2009/11/07/s...yroiditis-by-jeffrey-dach-md.aspx?view=linear](http://jeffreydach.com/2009/11/07/selenium-for-hashimotos-thyroiditis-by-jeffrey-dach-md.aspx?view=linear)
[http://jeffreydach.com/2011/09/14/iodine\_hashimotos.aspx](http://jeffreydach.com/2011/09/14/iodine_hashimotos.aspx)

There is a critical relationship between iodine & selenium. This is why Dr.Brownstein requires selenium with his iodine protocol. One without the other can cause thyroid problems.

If your doctor says Hashi, I suggest reading all of these references to better understand as much as possible about the condition. As I said, see our thyroid Bible first for anything related to thyroid problems. I have found this file to be the most honest & most valuable reference for all questions re: thyroid.

Grizz

JanSz,

What are your thoughts on this subject?

PS) The Elephant in the room for the thyroid: NO ONE discusses the impact of Bromide, Fluoride & Chlorine on the Thyroid. All we know for sure is that these toxins replace iodine in the thyroid (and in other organs), and I don't have any references to the impact. All I have is that the thyroid can be damaged by Fluoride. If you run into any doctor reports, please let me know. This could be another reason for Hashimotos.

This Report says both Hashi & Hypo are caused by Fluoride. But it is not a doctor report, so I can't refer to it: I want to see only doctors reporting before I can pass it along to others.
[http://www.fluoridefree.net/fluoride-causes-hypothyroidism.php](http://www.fluoridefree.net/fluoride-causes-hypothyroidism.php)

**I've just found confirmation to this link by Dr. Brownstein:**
Dr. Brownstein: Animal studies show that you cannot cause Hashimoto’s disease in an animal unless they are iodine deficient and they are given a goitrogen. That is what is happening to our human population; we are iodine deficient and we are exposed to an ever-increasing amount of goitrogens like bromide and fluoride.
Dr. Brownstein: Iodine can cause a detoxification reaction whereby the body releases bromide. I believe this is where many of the adverse effects are coming from. I explain this in my books. My experience has clearly shown that vast majority of patients with autoimmune thyroid disorders improve with iodine when it is used as part of a holistic treatment regimen.
Do some react negatively to iodine? Yes. Iodine is not for everyone, but, my clinical experience has clearly shown adverse effects to iodine are rare when it is used appropriately.

Dr. Eric: So then this should also be the case with hyperthyroidism and Graves’ Disease too, as while most of my patients with hyperthyroid conditions do fine supplementing with iodine, I have had a few who were deficient in iodine, yet didn’t feel great when first supplementing with it.

Dr. Brownstein: Absolutely. The entire country is in a state of iodine decline.
[http://www.naturalendocrinesolution...avid-brownstein-on-iodine-and-thyroid-health/](http://www.naturalendocrinesolutions.com/archives/an-interview-with-dr-david-brownstein-on-iodine-and-thyroid-health/)

### JanSz — 2013-12-10T01:29:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-103013>

> [Grizz said:](/goto/post?id=103010)
>
> \>> I don't have any information for low Temp in men when progesterone is low. You or Dr. Kruse are probably the experts on this subject.
>
> JanSz,
>
> What are your thoughts on this subject?
>
> Grizz

With progesterone I am going against a grain.
I am 73yo man.
There is little information available about progesterone as it pertains to man.
Most doctors skirt the issue and do not talk much about it.
If they talk it is usually to warn that it may hurt man.
Any doctor freely admits that women need lots of it.
I have seen dr Hertoghe presentation on progesterone and have his books.
Even dr Hertoghe is somewhat cautious when it comes to progesterone.

I am on testosterone replacement since 2003-4, after a while I have developed low cortisol, low progesterone, low pregnenolone, low DHEAs.
In retrospect I think I was lucky when couple doctors were unwilling to help me, and the first one told me to get ready to pass to the other side.
Low testosterone for me meant, no sex, depression, hysteria, close to loosing marriage.
Overall I was able to secure scripts for testing and medication but no medical advice. That was a very good deal, allowed me to learn and experiment on myself.

Supplementing low cortisol with HC or synthetic is popular advice but it rubs me wrong way because it is unnatural plus kept me busy looking at the clock all day.
Similarly with progesterone except available pills are too big, creams are cumbersome plus do not allow much contact with water.

High cholesterol, nobody knows what to do with it except for statins of Red Yeast Rice (which is really same thing)..
At first there was no connection DHEAs----cholesterol
Then I noted that when I count the weight of all major steroid hormones (those that are mostly tested), then in healthy person
95% is DHEAs
4.5% is cortisol
and 0.5% is everything else

That convinced me that I must come up with good DHEAs(500μg/dL)
Taking the recommended by most 10-50mg DHEA will not do the job.
I had to take 400mg/day for long time to get there, and surprise, cholesterol got down.

My cortisol I am raising to the top of morning good level 23 (6.2-19.4)ug/dL
to do that I am using pregnenolone Micronized Lipid Matrix from Nutricology.
At first I had to use 900mg/day after about 9 months I think I reached saturation point, had to go lower on dose.
For some time I got down to 300mg/day, but recently I had to raise it again to 400mg/day

I had to make decission about progesterone.
My pregnenolone is still low 108 ng/dL (<151)ng/dL
but progesterone goes thru the roof, at the max it was 4.4(<1.4 ng/mL) ( that is high even for women in first half of cycle)
but I had overal nothing but good news,
FT3/RT3=50, healthy ratio is 20 or greater.
T3/RT3=13.3, healthy ratio is 10 or greater.
sex ok, actually I have it to spare but DW have enough
then
I see lagging sex
progesterone is 5aR inhibitor, sex depends less on testosterone and more on DHT
I note that my DHT was higer but now is trending down.
Replaced one of my testosterone injections with 5grams of Androgel 1%, problem solved

\-----------------------------------------
How progesterone influences body temperature I do not know, but it does not hurt.

### Grizz — 2013-12-10T01:49:04+0200

nicld,
There are no easy answers for anything related to metabolism. Millions of people walk around with poor metabolism, cold hands & cold feet and feel OK. However they are not optimal. Normal for them is not optimal. Carefully read the work of Dr. Rind who I think gives the best explanation with charts:
[http://www.drrind.com/therapies/metabolic-temperature-graph](http://www.drrind.com/therapies/metabolic-temperature-graph)

**Our BIBLE probably provides the most comprehensive answers.**

> Dr. Broda Barnes, a doctor who paid attention to clinical presentation and prescribed the pre-reformulated Armour, found that a healthy before-rising morning basal temp should be between 97.8 – 98.2 (using a mercury thermometer under the arm for ten minutes). If it’s higher, you may be hyperthyroid, and if it’s lower, you are most likely hypothyroid. If using an oral thermometer in the mouth and want to compare it to the way Broda Barnes did the underarm, leave in your mouth for five minutes, then subtract 1/2 degree from your result to make it closer to underarm temp taking — the latter which is usually lower. Also MUCH more:
> [http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/temperature/](http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/temperature/)

**Iron Deficiency Anemia**
There are other factors that can affect metabolism:

> Low iron, or more specifically, low ferritin, is one of the most overlooked causes of low thyroid function. \[1\] Ferritin is the stored form of iron that is used by the cells and a better measure of available iron levels than serum iron. Iron is a component of multiple enzymes that are used to generate energy, so low iron means poorly functioning enzymes, and little energy output. Those with low iron also have low T4 and even lower T3 levels. \[12\] Iron deficiency also lowers thyroid peroxidase (TPO) activity. TPO is an iron-containing enzyme that initiates the first two steps in thyroid hormone synthesis. \[2\]
>
> Low ferritin can cause negative reaction like palpitations, nervousness, and anxiety in someone starting thyroid hormone replacement. More here:
> **Other Cofactors
> **
> Iodine, iron, selenium, zinc, and Vitamin A are all essential for normal thyroid hormone metabolism \[12,13,14\], and can be obtained from diet or daily multi-vitamins. More:
> [http://tiredthyroid.com/cofactors](http://tiredthyroid.com/cofactors).

I do have more references: See thyroid, hypothyroid, hyperthyroid, hashi
[http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Reading](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Reading)

Notice that in all of these discussions, all roads lead to iodine, the most basic requirement. **_So why do so many Endo doctors refuse to admit that iodine is a required mineral for the thyroid?_** This is crazy and makes no sense to me. I wish Dr. Kruse would chime in here. Is there a conspiracy to keep the waiting rooms filled? Have the drug companies gagged the doctors? What in the H is going on here? These Endos can't even determine that cold hands & cold feet means hypothyroid. [What are Endos?](http://www.healio.com/endocrinology/news/online/%7B6460f5f8-3dc6-48fe-a0ca-ddd8fc0e8e80%7D/what-is-an-endocrinologist?gclid=CLKB29C3pbsCFUsV7AodpyQAqQ)

Sorry, I didn't notice the PM and thanks for the heads up. If you don't mind, I'll answer your question here. Private messages don't help anyone viewing this thread.

Q) What can I do for an itchy Candida infection under the breast?
I suggest painting 2% Lugols iodine directly to the affected area. Paint one breast area one day, and the other breast area the next day. Then paint the iodine area with Grapeseed oil right after the iodine to prevent Dermatitis. Continue painting Alternate days until the infection is gone.

Q) What is the recommended iodine dosage for PCOS?
IMO, PCOS is the most difficult condition for Iodine supplements to resolve. Dr. Brownstein recommends titrating up to 100mg daily for all iodine deficiency disease. You are very lucky to not have any detox symptoms at your current level of 25mg. However detox has been known to happen at 50 mg and even at 100 mg. IMO, PCOS could take up to 24 months before it resolves. Fibrocystic Breasts typically resolve in less than 1 month. Please review our PCOS Testimonials [http://tinyurl.com/iodine-testimonials](http://tinyurl.com/iodine-testimonials)

Q) Will taking iodine trigger an allergy while eating shellfish?
Every Cell in the body requires iodine, and members of the Curezone routinely take 100mg and more with no allergy problems. If you develop an allergy, it will be caused by the shellfish, not the iodine. If you had an allergy to iodine, it would have showed up immediately when yu started. Allergy problems are not with the iodine, but with the fillers used in the iodoral tablets. If you were to develop an allergy (extremely rare), switch from iodoral to Lugols.

Candida is usually caused by Low Body pH.

### Grizz — 2014-01-01T19:55:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-104561>

Amazing Testimonial about SEVERE Prostate Problems cured with iodine:

> I've had all the usual extreme prostate symptoms for many years (I'm 59) and have had a diagnosis of BPH (enlargement) and prostatitis from age 35. In the last 5 years things started getting worse: blood in my semen and urine, blood leaking out up to 12 hours after sex, dry ejaculation: no semen released until hours after orgasm when it would leak out, testicle pain off and on, knife-like pain radiating into my penis for a split second, then about a second or two later it hits again, this can go on for several minutes, painful ejaculation (every time), urgency and frequency causing me to get up as often as 10 times in the night to piss (usually 3 or 4 times) despite fluid restriction. Intolerance to caffeine and alcohol - I've given them both up. I have tried all the herbal remedies, pomegranate, curcumin, lycopene, selenium, vitamins, minerals etc and nothing has worked. I could not tolerate palmetto due to headaches. I've spent many hundreds of dollars on avodart and flomax. I've had a prostate biopsy to rule out cancer because my PSA was 10 and a cystoscopy that showed the enlargement. The avodart lowered the PSA to 5 but the symptoms didn't improve very much. I had to stop the avodart because of back pain, stomach pain (acid), foot and hand pain and general weakness. I tried starting up again several times and the upper-mid back pain always comes back within a day. I've had about 5 very painful episodes of urinary retention and blockage. Recently the pain on the always-dry ejaculation had got so bad and the after-effects so nasty: very frequent painful urination, i.e. every 10 minutes for a few hours, and the leaking of blood and semen that I have started abstaining from sex and decided that I might have to give up sex forever.
>
> In my job I research medical topics for doctors and have access to very old medical journals (from the 1800s to the present). I found some very old references to the use of iodine in BPH. When I searched the internet I found a few guys (google prostate Grizz) had had amazing success with it, so I thought, I've tried everything else, why not give it a try. I looked into the history of iodine use and it was used for many conditions for at least 130 years and was considered the most important medicine a doctor could use (for many conditions). I didn't think it could be that dangerous considering the number of years it was used though I am aware modern doctors have been taught it's deadly. (It's actually a critically essential mineral but you can easily overdose.)
>
> I took 5 drops of 5 % tincture of iodine (containing iodine and potassium iodide) in a glass of water every morning. Do not take it at night because it is a stimulant. Read the warning below before you try this. 5 drops of 5% tincture is very roughly 25 mg (or 25,000 micrograms) of iodine which is a huge dose in medical terms, however hikers use roughly the same dose to sterilize a litre of pond water to drink without ill effects at least for short periods of time. I believe it is too large a dose to use for more than a few weeks without taking a big risk. If you have thyroid trouble even one dose might be too much. I also painted my balls with the pure tincture mixed with olive oil (as suggested on the internet) because it is absorbed through the skin and that is close to the prostate. That was a mistake because it burnt the skin after 2 days and it peeled off like a sunburn. I switched to iodine sold for supplement use in a glycerine base and that was very messy and delivered a much lower dose of iodine to my balls/prostate. The very first night after using the tincture on my balls everytime I awoke to piss, I had an erection (that hasn't happened for about 40 years during the night). I did not have any problems with erections before. The erections have continued almost every time I get up in the night now.
>
> I stopped painting after a week as it was messy. After less than a week - I think it was about 4 or 5 days of the oral doses and ball painting, I decided to masturbate. I couldn't believe the result. I shot semen 10 inches or so with no pain, no blood, no leaking afterward, no frequent urge to piss etc. I hadn't had a wet orgasm in the daytime in about 5 years. My prostate feels much much better, I don't get the pain when urinating and the stream is stronger. I'm only getting up once or twice a night to piss. I can go for hours when I'm out and hold my bladder if I need to, without pain. I have only been taking the oral iodine for 3 weeks. All the symptoms improved after less than a week.
>
> WARNING: As this is a very high dose and could damage my thyroid, I plan to drop down to the daily recommended dose of 150 micrograms soon and I hope that the lower dose will continue to work. The risk of overdosing with iodine is very high so I do not recommend anyone do what I did without doctor's supervision - you literally could die in what is called a thyrotoxic storm or you could permanently damage your thyroid requiring radioactive iodine to kill your thyroid and then thyroid medication for the rest of your life. There are some doctors out there who are promoting iodine such as Dr. Flechas, Abraham and Brownstein for many conditions including breast cancer and have written books and papers on it. The latest conventional medicine paper I've seen (2012) showed that at a dose of only 400 micrograms per day (about 1/50th of what I'm taking), in people with normal thyroid function, 5 % developed subclinical hypothyroidism after only a few weeks. Most subjects returned to normal after stopping the iodine. It is not clear what the effect would be in iodine deficient people or people with hyper or hypothyroidism. The paper is free online here: [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/)... You could try a safer lower dose of iodine at 150 - 400 micrograms (mcg) per day. It might take longer to work of course, or it might not work at all. Also, the larger dose that I took might not work at all either. Your mileage may vary.
>
> Conclusion: I am a very happy man. I'm sorry for all the men out there who've received the same advice and treatment as I have and are suffering terribly, because modern medicine abandons it's own history and so doctors today and their patients will not know about this possible treatment. Interesting question: could iodine help prevent or treat prostate cancer? The Japanese consume much larger amounts of iodine than North Americans (around 12 mg per day - a very large dose) and breast and prostate cancer are much much lower in Japan. It is obvious that iodine with required co-suppplements are a miracle beyond our wildest imaginations.

Who Stole Our Iodine? It used to be a MAJOR medicine to treat many diseases:

**Special Report by Gabriel Cousens, MD, MD(H), DD**

-   In 1911, 900 milligrams daily were considered usual and safe doses.
    In 1950 the Japanese had 100 times more iodine!in!their diet than Americans. In 2001 they had 202 times more iodine than Americans and were using up to 13.8 milligrams daily!
-

-   A shocking 96% deficiency in Americans

-   Iodine is found in every single one of our body’s hundred trillion cells. Without! adequate iodine levels life is impossible.

-   Iodine is the universal health nutrient that brings health on many levels.

-   Higher dosages were used in 1917 through 1922, when Dr.David Marine proved that iodine reversed goiter in a study in which 2,000 school girls were given an equivalent of 18.6 milligrams daily (18,600 micrograms day) for 2!½ years with a dramatic success rate.

-   In 2003 Dr. Zhang showed that 100 mg potassium iodide reversed lung cancer tumors in mice.

-   In 1993, Dr. Ghent administered to 1,368 patients 5 milligrams (5,000 micrograms) daily with no evidence of the WolfSChaikoff theoretical problem of hypothyroidism.

-   The Iodine Project, from 1997 through 2005, Dr.Abraham, Dr.Brownstein, and Dr. Flechas followed 4,000 patients and administered 12.5 to 100 milligrams daily, with 100 milligrams administered to diabetics primarily, because low thyroid function is also associated with Type 2 diabetes. They had positive results with only three adverse reactions out of 4,000 people (possibly allergic reactions to the binding agents, excipients, fillers, preservatives or synthetics commonly found in tablets, capsules as opposed to the bioavailable form of iodine itself).
-

-   Glands/organs/systems! with high iodine uptake are the breasts, ovaries, cervix, blood, lymph, bones, gastric mucosal, salivary, adrenal, prostate, colon, thymus, lungs, bladder, kidney, and skin.

-   There are more than twenty two functions of iodine! These important! functions!include- Read report for details

[http://www.gabrielcousens.com/LinkC...ghQRidoLyU=&tabid=364&mid=6700&language=en-US](http://www.gabrielcousens.com/LinkClick.aspx?fileticket=LghQRidoLyU%3d&tabid=364&mid=6700&language=en-US)

**High Doses in History - 1930’s**
We have to revisit the 1930s when iodine was still a universal medicine, present in the US Pharmacopeia and was used at much higher dosages than anyone even dreams of using today.The usual dose for treatment was 300 mgs (46 drops of full strength Lugol’s) to 1 gm (1000 mg, 154 drops). It is very important to realize that today’s Lugol’s is not universally the same as it was because of new federal legal requirements about concentration levels, where Lugols is regulated to 2% & 5%

[http://drsircus.com/medicine/iodine/iodine-dosages](http://drsircus.com/medicine/iodine/iodine-dosages)

" Iodine is most important for women, because it is most highly concentrated in the thyroid, breasts and ovaries. Iodine deficiency can lead to menstrual irregularities, infertility, early menopause, and ovarian diseases. But it is still vital for men, especially for the prostate.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-04-22T13:56:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-122991>

DHEA = IL 6 = sleep = autophagy efficiency..........when it is bad you are losing electrons in a big way...........when autophagy is bad mitochondria are swelling and you are using more apoptosis which is mitochondrial suicide and not renewal........autophagy is how mitochondria reorganize and renew.

When you lose one electron in mito =superoxide. This is OK because it is a signal of what to do
When your lose two electrons in mito = H2O2 can be OK or bad depending on context
When you lose three of more in mito - Fenton reaction or a build up of hydroxyl free radicals........always bad. This causes massive tissue damage = low DHEA

Read the blog about Fenton reactions. Quantum Autism has a real detailed area on this where I discuss this.

Normally the only place a normal hydroxyl free radical is used in biology is in macrophages that kill bacteria. This occurs within the confines of these cells and not in tissues.
When they occur in tissues constantly due to non native EMF collagen is destroyed and unzipped.
When Non native EMF interacts with "dislodged transition metal from un zipped collagen it cause the Fenton reaction to occur. This is why unzipping proteins is a big problem in our modern world. It frees the transition metal to interact with the higher powered non native EMF. What happens? the energy in the EMF is transferred to the electrons in the D shell of these metals to cause ionization of the metal or the remnant unzipped protein fragments......this changes their chemistry in you.........and it makes hydroxyl free radicals we can not clear well.

The hydroxyl radical can damage virtually all types of macromolecules by energizing their molecular structures using the quanta of energy to alter its structure and function: This is true of proteins, carbohydrates, nucleic acids (mutations), lipids (lipid peroxidation) and amino acids coded for by DNA and RNA.

The hydroxyl radical has a very short _in vivo_ half-life of approximately 10−9 seconds and a high reactivity. This makes it a very dangerous compound to any living organisms mitochondrial membranes or its cell membranes. It causes you to lose electrons in massive numbers.

Unlike superoxide, which can be detoxified by superoxide dismutase, the hydroxyl radical **cannot** be eliminated by an enzymatic reaction. Mechanisms for scavenging peroxyl radicals for the protection of cellular structures includes endogenous antioxidants such as melatonin and glutathione.

This is precisely how non native EMF destroys your redox potential everywhere.

### Melanie Procter — 2014-05-04T04:56:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-124944>

I was reading up on iodine more as my African friends were telling me about an epidemic of fibroids in women throughout Africa.I then seared the blog for "fibroids". I found Dr. Kruse recommended iodine. I was then trying to figure out the fibroid/iodine connection. I found this on the Hashi's blog.

"[Iodine](http://www.ameerrosic.com/why-i-gained-30-pounds-in-90-days-thyroid-problems-in-women/) is critical for oxidation protection of [PUFA](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PUFA)’s utilized by the [brain and thyroid](http://www.ameerrosic.com/why-i-gained-30-pounds-in-90-days-thyroid-problems-in-women/), such as [DHA](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Docosahexaenoic_acid). It also appears that iodine is a lot better protector of all [lipids](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lipids) in all cells and not just the brain. With [iodine deficiency,](http://www.ameerrosic.com/why-i-gained-30-pounds-in-90-days-thyroid-problems-in-women/) the body loses the ability to handle [ROS](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reactive_oxygen_species) optimally, and other organ systems have to offset those losses to protect the cell from more oxidation. Supplementation is not the answer."

Would you say the way iodine deficiency is like having a house without insulation? If that is the case, I wonder if the epidemic of fibroids is due to massive electron loss from issues like we read in the last blog on the gut. Or do you think that that the fibroid epidemic in Africa is connected to some sort of methylation issue due to Africans eating a diet not native to Africa? I think it is a bit of both.Most Africans eat a lot of bread, but bread is still very new to Africa.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-05-04T17:05:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-125004>

Iodine is one of 7 nonmetals exist as diatomic molecules in their standard states. Iodine however has some interesting emergent properties. In crystalline form, is semi-lustrous, and a semiconductor in the direction of its layers, both of these attributes being consistent with incipient metallic character. It is also a great insulator. This makes it idea to work in neural synapses to protect DHA and to augment electron tunneling across the synapse. Because iodine has a higher atomic number than the bad halogens, it can intercalate between DHA in synapses to semiconduct. This is how thyroid hormones work as well. Iodine also forms strong hydrogen binding networks which means it works well with water. Iodine most important affect with DHA is its strong electron affinity.......it helps move electrons from the pi electron cloud between DHA molecules at synapses.

Anything "massless" in nature is found in the human brain. Massless bosons are photons and solitons. Electrons are fermions. I wrote the following in my Quantum Brain blog. Solitons, massless bosons or coherent polarization waves are likely all synonymous sources of energy. We know from diffusion weighted MRI that neurons swell just prior to activation. This swelling could begin a soliton in the brain just like an earthquake begins a tsunami in the ocean. In deep water one never realizes the size or power of the wave, but as the depth and thickness of the water column changes the waves power gets bigger. From the brain’s cortical mantle to the outflow white matter tracts the diameter of the pathways of CSF narrow dramatically. This anatomic arrangement is ideal for generation of solitons. We know experimentally from physics that these types of soliton waves can be confined and propagated as beams of energy to be 15 nanometers in diameter. This also happens to be the **exact inner diameter** of the microtubules everywhere in our body. This is a massively important size relationship to remember as the blog rolls on. I do not think this situation is a happen-chance coincidence at all. I think it is part of quantum fractal design. It is what forms the power to evolve consciousness in human life. We stimulate solitons when we touch or massage our tissues. Modern research shows that relatively long-lived dark solitons have been produced and studied extensively even in biology. Tsunami’s are a great example of a soliton in nature.

The electron rest mass (symbol: me) is the mass of a stationary electron. It is one of the fundamental constants of physics, and is also very important in chemistry because of its relation to the Avogadro constant. It has a value of about 9.11×10−31 kilograms or about 5.486×10−4 atomic mass units, equivalent to an energy of about 8.19×10−14 joules or about 0.511 megaelectronvolts. The brain is designed to remove the mass from an electron and change it to photon. When this happens what is the phase transition that happens? A photon or quanta of energy is released to the system. ### Melanie Procter — 2014-05-12T05:11:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-iodine-thread.943/post-126394>

I found this just now.
"What makes an estrogen good or bad? That has to do with the biological activity, or potency, of that estrogen. Estrogens are important in a host of cellular activities that affect growth and differentiation in various target cells. This is normal and beneficial, but too much estrogenic stimulation can have a negative effect. Therefore, properly metabolizing and excreting estrogens is crucial. This is how the daughter compounds differ substantially. If these estrogens are metabolized into the 2-hydroxylated estrone and estradiol, they lose much of their cell proliferative and estrogenic activity and are termed "good" estrogen metabolites. Studies show that when 2-hydroxylation increases, the body resists cancer, and that when 2-hydroxylation decreases, cancer risk increases."

My guess is increased T3 improves estrogen metabolism thus foods like arame would be a big part of a bad estrogen detox.
